claim: Official PCSX2 master 86d76bbf590566d9ea74d381eeff3acd9856503a built as 2.7.485 Release AVX2 with zero errors and warnings.
The owned NTSC-U image boots as SCUS-97264 with CRC D5605611 and ELF entry 0x00100008.
The owned disc image SHA-256 is A60A4B41FEA808335BAA3B887A377BB98063F6F169CA498EFEE49397FBA96130.
SCUS_972.64 is ELF32 little-endian MIPS with five program headers and eleven sections.
The common HOG directory layout validates across 273 top-level archives and 32351 entries with zero structural errors.
The MINSK token occurs at executable file offset 0x003ACBC8 and mapped virtual address 0x004ACAC8, and GAMEDATA/MINSK exists on disc.
The retail -x option is a skip-intro or direct-level-enable switch.
The independent native C++ HOG index validates 273 archives, 32351 entries, and 3409763624 bytes with zero errors without loading payloads into memory.
The independently written SDL3 host initializes the Direct3D 12 GPU backend and renders 120 frames before a clean automatic exit.
The retail argument parser recognizes attached -l<level> values; MINSK is table index 9, and the loader constructs level-specific then common LOADING.HOG paths.
Across 118 retail .SO modules, the custom little-endian container parser reaches exact EOF, validates 4950 strictly ordered in-bounds local callable entries, and finds no ELF modules.

The passive asset fingerprint corpus covers 6677 nested HOGs and identifies stable structural families for TDX, SKM, SKL, VAG, LPD, PAR, COL, VUM, POP, and map-associated data without publishing payloads.

The retail -x option sets an independent startup mode used by widget-backed versus timed splash selection and one first-character construction branch during DATA.HOG parsing; it does not select or mount the level.

The bounded startup trace verifies localized-string loading, level then common LOADING.HOG fallback, DATA.HOG mounting with thirteen ordered parser calls, and later level/common weapon and NPC archive mounts.
The independent native range index validates all 6677 nested HOG spans: 501 exact and 6176 with verified all-zero tails, with zero errors.
The independent native POP terrain-prefix parser validates all 18 level POP files and 5351 terrain records, including 4144 records with nonzero alignment bytes, with zero errors.
The independent native level-manifest adapter resolves all 5351 POP terrain references across all 18 levels to canonical DATA.HOG members by normalized basename stem, with zero errors.
The independent passive native descriptor corpus validates 7036 COL, 7036 VUM, and 15248 TDX spans with zero errors and reproduces every aggregate extent class without decoding payload semantics.

The pure-native startup validates the owner-supplied NTSC-U data root, loads all 18 levels and 5351 canonical manifest cells, and renders the 299-cell MINSK synthetic coverage grid through SDL_GPU/D3D12 for 120 frames without a lingering process.

The bounded native COL adapter decodes all 7036 owned-corpus spans into canonical spatial meshes with zero errors: 23913 source nodes, 99193 leaves, 1327714 triangles, 949762 vertices, 2716 normalized empty meshes, 2678 direct leaf roots, and maximum edge depth 8.

The native content service resolves all 5351 manifest cells across all 18 levels into 5351 owned spatial meshes with zero errors: 20203 canonical nodes, 93356 leaves, 889640 vertices, 1239980 triangles and references, and 2137 normalized empty meshes; native startup independently preserves 5351/5351 manifest-spatial cardinality.
check: build/msvc/Debug/omega_tool.exe level-spatial-verify-tree private/extracted-disc; python -B tools/probe_native_levels.py build/msvc/Debug/openomega.exe private/extracted-disc

claim: Superseding the TDX portion of E-0018, the semantic native TDX adapter decodes all 15248 owned-corpus spans with zero errors into 15442 source-order blocks, 17960 primary planes, 285521272 owned primary bytes, 15190 palette blocks, 252 direct blocks, and 1510240 source-order palette entries; 62 textures normalize exactly 4112 duplicate-proven zero suffix bytes.
check: build/msvc/Debug/omega_tool.exe asset-metadata-verify-tree private/extracted-disc; python -B tools/prove_tdx_zero_suffix.py private/extracted-disc

claim: Superseding the VUM portion of E-0018, the semantic native VUM adapter decodes all 7036 owned-corpus spans with zero errors into 38793 source-order owned names, 38899 material records, and 42631 dense name references while validating and discarding 220943 bounded P/Q/T metadata records.

The retail-only passive native VUM render-payload descriptor validates all 7036 owned-corpus spans with zero errors: 91460 source-order Q/P pairs, 38023 normalized T target ordinals, 134122 middle-to-final references, 365840 ordered Q/P final references, and middle-span counts of 48798 at 16 bytes, 35408 at 256 bytes, 3556 at 480 bytes, and 3698 at 704 bytes; it retains no payload bytes or render geometry.

Superseding the diagnostic-view portion of E-0019, native startup builds its bounded source-order wireframe contact sheet from canonical LevelSpatialIR rather than manifest metadata; synthetic integration holds manifest records constant and proves that changed COL topology changes the owned RGBA8 pixels.

Aggregate structural scanning proves that all 213 SKA spans satisfy a neutral 112-byte-prefix counted-word extent: 158 are exact and 55 have only zero padding; the two distinct SKAS candidates satisfy a bounded printable-ASCII CRLF text envelope. No animation, timing, transform, channel, compression, label, value, or relationship semantics are assigned.

The fixed-output retail-only SKA descriptor validates all 213 owned-corpus spans with zero errors: 158 exact, 55 zero-padded, and 2180832 aggregate logical bytes. It retains no payload bytes and assigns no actor, skeleton, timing, channel, transform, compression, or animation semantics; SKAS remains separate.

The privacy-safe bounded POP post-terrain scanner accepts all 18 owned-corpus level POPs with zero errors and finds 342 aligned literal-tag candidate hits: the same 19 candidates occur exactly once per file in one shared order. This confirms only a literal envelope; it assigns no section boundary, header, count, extent, placement, visibility, or field semantics.

The bounded aggregate TDX scorer accepts all 15248 owned-corpus spans and scores 2176 direct indexed planes with zero errors. Among 2014 four-bit 0x14 planes, low-nibble-first has 1765 plane wins, 139 losses, 110 ties, and the lower source-channel adjacency delta sum (93917821 versus 109066377). Among 162 eight-bit 0x13 planes, the bit-3/bit-4 palette permutation has 145 wins, 7 losses, 10 ties, and the lower sum (554076 versus 658936). These content-dependent measurements nominate hypotheses only and do not confirm nibble, palette, channel, swizzle, mip, or display semantics.

The bounded aggregate POP layout scorer accepts all 18 owned-corpus POPs with zero errors and tests 1443 bounded nonzero candidate count words across 342 marker spans. Five marker-relative +4 word/fixed-stride tuples fit every nonzero occurrence tested: INL:/36 bytes (18/18), PNT:/88 (17/17), DIR:/44 (18/18), ENV:/76 (18/18), and INV:/84 (16/16). Zero words are excluded from stride evidence. These exact arithmetic measurements nominate hypotheses only and do not confirm markers, counts, records, boundaries, placement, visibility, or field semantics.

Extending E-0030 with separate zero-word accounting, the same five marker-relative +4 word/fixed-stride candidate formulas fit all 18 owned-corpus occurrences each. INL:/36, DIR:/44, and ENV:/76 have 18 nonzero exact fits; PNT:/88 has 17 nonzero fits plus one zero word with the predicted empty extent; INV:/84 has 16 nonzero fits plus two zero words with predicted empty extents. Zero cases add no stride evidence. The formulas remain arithmetic hypotheses and assign no boundary, record, placement, visibility, or field semantics.

The bounded POP candidate record-shape profiler accepts all 18 owned-corpus POPs and all 90 guarded candidate-formula occurrences with zero errors. It classifies 8019 candidate records and 105985 anonymous four-byte column observations: INL: 5351 records across 9 columns, PNT: 2358 across 22, DIR: 43 across 11, ENV: 65 across 19, and INV: 202 across 21. These aggregate bit-pattern measurements confirm only the exact candidate arithmetic envelope; they assign no decoded section, count, record, field type, placement, visibility, or gameplay semantics.

The public-safe level material-catalog verifier accepts all 18 owned-corpus levels and all 5351 manifest-referenced cells/catalogs with zero errors, preserving manifest order and producing 34267 owned names, 34589 material records, and 37893 dense name references. This confirms bounded GameDataService archive traversal, cumulative decoding, ownership, and cardinality only; it assigns no name role, COL/TDX binding, placement, visibility, or render semantics.

The first privacy-safe bounded VUM consumer trace produced one complete 120-frame pair from one selected runtime copy. Strict validation accepted the pair and its byte-identical repeat; each validated report contains 2 EE-read aggregate rows, 2 anonymous-site rows, and 0 VIF1 chunk rows. An independently guarded second ranked trial reproduced those aggregates exactly: one capture with 4 accepted aggregate rows split evenly between EE-read and anonymous-site rows, 0 VIF1 rows, and zero aggregate-count deltas. Across both trials, the EE-read rows remain confined to the already-opaque header-vector block; no accepted row reaches counts, records, metadata, payload, VIF, or tail data. Post-run containment auditing found no retained runtime copy, executable surface, reparse point, owner-input copy, or emulator/build process. This repeated header-only result does not exclude copied buffers or activity outside the bounded observation window and assigns no geometry, topology, vertex, material, packet, draw, placement, visibility, or gameplay semantics.
check: Strict-validate both reports for each accepted trial against the out-of-tree expected span bound, require raw byte identity, and compare only sanitized aggregate counts.

claim: The native all-level startup probe accepts all 18 owned-corpus levels and all 5351 manifest-referenced cells with zero errors after loading one all-or-error LevelContentIR per level. GameDataService traverses each common/nested archive and referenced cell once, cumulatively budgets the unique COL and VUM decoders, preserves manifest order and cardinality, and retains both canonical collections as owned state. This confirms orchestration, budgeting, ownership, and cardinality only; it assigns no mesh-to-material binding, placement, visibility, or render semantics.

The bounded aggregate common-archive TDX containment scan accepts all 18 owned-corpus runtime levels, 5351 manifest cell occurrences, and 5413 scanned DATA.HOG-graph archive-directory occurrences, including 44 deeper-container occurrences, with zero errors, normalized collisions, malformed TDX inputs, or normalized .TDX-suffixed members. All 5351 manifest cell occurrences are likewise zero-TDX in this extension-bounded scope. This does not exclude texture data embedded under another suffix or representation and assigns no ownership or texture-to-cell, name, material, mesh, draw, placement, visibility, or render relationship.

The bounded aggregate direct sibling-container scan accepts both explicit container classes for all 18 owned-corpus runtime levels: 36 exact containers and 5801 direct TDX members with zero errors, malformed textures, normalized collisions, nested traversal, or non-TDX entries. The generic primary and map classes contribute 5765 and 36 occurrences. This confirms direct containment, not runtime ownership, necessity, priority, or a texture-to-cell, name, material, mesh, draw, placement, visibility, mip, or render relationship; exact native composed Open/Load item and logical-output maxima remain measurement gaps.

Superseding the native-measurement-gap portion of E-0037, the public-safe native LevelTextureStore verifier accepts all 18 owned-corpus runtime levels, 36 explicit sources, and 5801 level-inventory texture occurrences with zero errors. It loads 5913 blocks, 7603 planes, 615232 palette entries, 27101352 plane bytes, 2460928 palette bytes, and 29562280 owned bytes. Independent field maxima are Open input/items/logical-output/depth/scratch 3076944/1460/111014/0/71467 and Load 3139344/5169/333232/0/65595; no complete tuple is asserted to co-occur. This confirms bounded native Open/Load integration and owned storage occurrences only, not unique whole-disc identity, retail ownership, display layout, material/cell/mesh/draw binding, placement, visibility, or rendering semantics. The report emits no paths, names, hashes, offsets, payloads, per-level rows, identities, or bindings.

The native all-level startup probe accepts all 18 owned-corpus levels and all 5351 manifest-referenced cells with zero errors after named-level startup opens and retains an inventory-only LevelTextureStore following manifest, all-or-error LevelContentIR, and synthetic debug-image success. Synthetic regressions confirm no-level startup leaves the store disengaged, malformed texture-container Open failures retain their typed LevelTextures error without partial publication, and moving the complete startup state preserves the store-to-service binding and handle identity. Startup performs no texture Load, display expansion, material/name/cell/mesh/draw binding, placement, visibility, GPU upload, or rendering.
check: ctest --preset msvc-debug; python -B tools/probe_native_levels.py build/msvc/Debug/openomega.exe private/extracted-disc --aggregate-only

claim: The retail-only passive native POP post-terrain hypothesis descriptor and fixed-schema verifier discover 18 owned-corpus regular .POP candidates, accept all 18, reject none, and report zero errors. Each accepted descriptor fail-closed validates only the previously established TER-to-GOB prefix, ordered aligned-literal envelope, and five marker-relative +4 word/fixed-stride arithmetic extents. Independent accepted-only input/items/logical-output/string/scratch maxima are 919360/1/168/26/80036; each field is maximized separately and no complete tuple is asserted to co-occur. The fixed owned descriptor retains no input span or payload and is not canonical asset IR. Native acceptance confirms implementation conformity to the prior arithmetic hypotheses only; it does not confirm section boundaries, count or record semantics, payload interpretation, placement, visibility, rendering, or gameplay. The aggregate emits no paths, names, hashes, literal spellings, candidate offsets, observed words, strides, opaque-range sizes, per-file rows, identities, or bindings.

The bounded analysis-only full-normalized-member-name lexical-coherence rerun scans all 18 owned-corpus runtime levels with zero errors. Its 5351 manifest cell, 5351 VUM catalog, 34267 name, 34589 material-record, 37893 dense-reference, 36 explicit-container, and 5801 class-qualified locator occurrence totals exactly match the independently confirmed native GameDataService and LevelTextureStore populations; this is validation-scope/cardinality parity, not a native lookup result. Independent names/materials/references-per-catalog, locators-per-level, and candidate-locators-per-name maxima are 140/140/142/730/0. All 34267 VUM name occurrences and all 37893 dense references classify as lacking terminal .TDX; invalid, unmatched eligible exact, unique-primary, unique-map, and ambiguous-cross-class counts are zero. All 34589 material records have an ineligible reference, and all 5801 class-qualified locator occurrences remain unreached. Equality compares only complete safely normalized strings when the VUM name ends exactly in .TDX; it does not test basename, stem, extension removal, suffix substitution, nested-container, fuzzy, or other alias families. The narrow negative result does not establish that catalog names are texture names, that material records bind textures, that retail code performs the lookup, that container class implies priority, or that runtime integration is justified. The fixed report emits no paths, names, hashes, offsets, payloads, per-level rows, locator identities, or inferred semantics.

The separate bounded analysis-only exact-first one-terminal-extension lexical-coherence rerun scans all 18 owned-corpus runtime levels twice with byte-identical fixed-schema-version-2 output and zero errors. It retains E-0041's 5351 manifest cell, 5351 VUM catalog, 34267 name, 34589 material-record, 37893 dense-reference, 36 explicit-container, and 5801 original class-qualified direct-.TDX locator occurrence totals. After complete normalization and only when full strings have no candidate, it independently removes at most one syntactic extension from the final component on both sides, preserves directory components, and compares exactly once more; the direct .TDX locator universe remains unchanged. All 34267 names and 37893 dense references classify as extension-elided unique-primary candidates, with every other status zero. All 34589 material records have all references unique, have a unique candidate, and have an extension-elided candidate; unmatched, ambiguous, and ineligible material flags are zero. Of the original locator occurrences, 5690 are reached only through extension elision and 111 remain unreached; exact-only and exact-and-extension-elided coverage are zero. Independent names/materials/references-per-catalog, locators-per-level, and candidate-locators-per-name maxima are 140/140/142/730/1. This is offline lexical candidate evidence, not observation that retail code removes extensions, performs the lookup, or consumes a material record. It does not establish that catalog names are texture names, that materials bind textures, that container class implies priority, that locators bind cells, meshes, draws, placement, visibility, or renderer resources, or that runtime integration is justified; basename, stem, substring, fuzzy, repeated-extension, suffix-family, nested-container, indirection, alternate-representation, and other alias behavior remain untested. The fixed report emits no paths, names, hashes, offsets, payloads, transformed keys, per-level rows, locator identities, or inferred semantics.

The native asynchronous AssetService v0 uses a fixed reusable generation-handle slot pool, explicit Ready/Failed release, bounded in-flight and resident-logical accounting, and JobService workers that retain a shared implementation through callable return; destruction stops acceptance, waits this service's accepted work, and expires handles. OmegaApp owns it optionally only when LevelTextureStore exists and destroys it before JobService and content dependencies. A clean MSVC build completed with zero warnings and errors, focused checks and the full 18/18 CTest suite passed, and 100 repeated lifecycle runs passed. Two owned-tree schema-version-1 verifier passes were byte-identical and accepted 18 levels, 36 sources, and 5801 texture occurrences with zero errors. Occurrences, requests, Ready observations, successful Gets, releases, stale-handle rejections, and zero-residual checks each total 5801. Storage totals are 5913 blocks, 7603 planes, 615232 palette entries, 27101352 plane bytes, 2460928 palette bytes, and 29562280 owned bytes; maxima are one active slot, one in-flight request, and 333232 resident logical bytes. The verifier uses one worker, one pending job, and service limits of 1/1/524288; internal defaults are 64 slots, 64 in-flight requests, and 64 MiB resident logical output, with a hard 8192-slot maximum. These are synthetic project bounds, not retail limits or user settings. The service establishes no VUM-name/material lookup, alias rule, material/texture/cell/mesh/draw binding, display pixels, GPU upload, placement, visibility, or rendering. The fixed aggregate emits no paths, names, hashes, offsets, payloads, per-level rows, identities, bindings, messages, or exception text. The unchanged E-0038 verifier was revalidated.

The SDL-free native RenderTexturePool foundation preallocates fixed metadata slots, validates exact overflow-checked tightly packed project-owned RGBA8 extents, and provides transactional Reserve, Publish, and Rollback for future backend work. Unique nonzero process-local pool identities plus slot and 64-bit generation values reject default, foreign, stale, and released handles; explicit release refunds logical resident bytes, and a maximum generation retires rather than wraps. Defaults are 64 slots and 64 MiB logical RGBA8 with a hard 8192-slot maximum. RenderFramePacket now carries a default-invalid fixed-width diagnostic texture handle while remaining trivially copyable and standard layout. A clean MSVC build completed with zero warnings and errors, the focused test passed, 100 repeated focused runs passed, and the full 19/19 CTest suite passed. This confirms only portable renderer metadata and lifecycle policy. The pool creates no GPU resource; SdlGpuHost and OmegaApp do not consume the new handle; the existing one-off debug upload remains unchanged; and no GPU upload, blit, allocation, synchronization, or residency behavior and no AssetService, TDX, VUM, material, binding, display-pixel, placement, visibility, or rendering semantic is established.

The native SDL generated-RGBA8 integration supersedes E-0044's historical host non-consumption and unchanged one-off-upload state. SdlGpuHost owns a fixed slot-indexed SDL texture table parallel to RenderTexturePool. Upload is an RAII transaction across Reserve, backend texture and transfer creation, copy-command submission, and Publish; failure rolls back portable residency and acquired backend resources. Release waits for GPU idle. RenderFramePacket carries the resident generation selected for blitting, while the default-invalid handle selects clearing; OmegaApp uploads its existing project-generated diagnostic image, stores only the handle, and explicitly releases it before host fallback teardown. Aggregate snapshots contain no pool, texture, source, or backend identities. Post-swapchain command-buffer unwinding submits instead of taking SDL's illegal cancel path. Pool defaults remain 64 slots and 64 MiB logical RGBA8 with a hard 8192-slot maximum. A clean MSVC build completed with zero warnings and errors, and the default full 19/19 CTest suite passed. One initial plus 20 repeated public zero-file GPU smokes all passed on direct3d12, 21 total, and a public two-frame openomega smoke passed. Every GPU smoke uses capacity 1 and a 256-byte budget; performs one successful clear-only submission; uploads, blits, and releases opaque 8x8 A at 256 bytes; rejects A's stale handle before GPU access; uploads, blits, and releases opaque 4x8 B at 128 bytes in the same slot with a new generation; and checks idle. Exact totals are 2 uploads, 384 cumulative logical bytes, 2 releases, 2 blits, 1 clear-only submission, 1 rejection, 0 unavailable-swapchain submissions, and final capacity 1/free 1 with zero reserved, resident, retired, or charged bytes. The smoke proves command submission and checked idle, not framebuffer pixel identity or readback. The executable always compiles when testing and the SDL backend are enabled, but hardware/display CTest registration is off by default and opt-in through OMEGA_RUN_GPU_SMOKE_TEST. This establishes no TextureStorageIR or AssetService bridge, TDX plane or palette consumption, channel/alpha/nibble/palette/swizzle/mip/display expansion, VUM/material/alias/binding, scene placement/visibility, retail rendering, gameplay, measured GPU bytes, streaming/eviction, asynchronous upload, or fence design.

The native renderer now consumes an immutable owned RenderDrawList with a fixed maximum of 16 source-order texture-blit commands and 65536-unit normalized target rectangles. Creation is allocation-free, validates every handle as structurally nondefault and every rectangle, preserves duplicates and order, zeroes the inactive tail, and exposes only a const prefix span; the rectangle, command, list, and RenderFramePacket remain trivially copyable and standard layout. A pure overflow-safe integer planner maps normalized rectangles to aspect-contained pixel destinations using floor starts and ceil ends. RenderFramePacket owns the list by value, while commands neither own nor pin texture residency. Before acquiring a command buffer, SdlGpuHost resolves every listed handle into fixed storage, so a stale later handle rejects the whole frame without rendering an accepted prefix. An empty list performs a full-target clear; a nonempty list first clears the full swapchain target and then submits every blit in source order with load preservation and nearest sampling. Aggregate counters distinguish submitted nonempty frames from successful draws and expose no resource identities. OmegaApp retains its generated diagnostic texture handle solely for explicit release and separately builds one full-target draw list. A clean MSVC build completed with zero warnings and errors, the focused portable test plus 100 repeated runs passed, and the default full 20/20 CTest suite passed. One initial plus 20 repeated public zero-file GPU smokes all passed on direct3d12. Every smoke used capacity 2 and a 384-byte logical-residency budget and finished with exact totals of 3 uploads, 640 cumulative logical upload bytes, 3 releases, 2 submitted blit frames, 4 successful draws, 1 clear-only submission, 1 stale-handle rejection, 0 unavailable-swapchain submissions, all slots free, and zero reserved, resident, retired, or charged state. The opt-in GPU test also passed as the twenty-first registered CTest, after which its option was restored off and the default suite returned to 20 tests. A public two-frame openomega smoke with dummy audio also passed. This confirms bounded immutable draw submission, preflight handle validation, command ordering, checked GPU submission, and idle resource cleanup; it does not prove arbitrary backend-failure atomicity, framebuffer pixel identity or readback, asynchronous lifetime pinning, fences, streaming or eviction, measured GPU memory, TextureStorageIR or AssetService consumption, TDX plane or palette or display expansion, channel, alpha, nibble, palette, swizzle, mip, VUM, material, alias, cell, mesh, placement, visibility, retail rendering, or gameplay semantics.
